_comment: Model routing for environments that support per-agent model switching. Only Claude Code and OpenClaw can actually switch models - Codex uses persona-switching with a single model.
codex_note: Codex is not included in environments because it cannot switch models per agent. It uses persona-switching with a single model for the entire conversation. The tier assignments in the agents section show INTENDED capability levels for documentation purposes only.

speaker.description: Lightweight orchestrator — routes tasks, synthesizes results, communicates with the Spirit
speaker.rationale: Routing and coordination only. Deep reasoning not required. Fast, low-cost models are appropriate.

checker.description: Mechanical verification — confirms tasks have corresponding implementations
checker.rationale: Pattern matching and verification. Fast and cheap is optimal.

planer.description: Strategic vision — maintains project coherence, guards against scope creep
planer.rationale: Planning needs reasoning about dependencies and interactions. Balanced models sufficient.

tasker.description: Task decomposition — translates plan features into discrete, measurable tasks
tasker.rationale: Requires judgment about ambiguity and measurability. Balanced models appropriate.

prompter.description: Prompt optimization — reduces token cost, improves caching, streamlines communication
prompter.rationale: Meta-level understanding of LLM text processing. Balanced models handle this well.

deployer.description: DevOps and deployment — scripts, CI/CD, infrastructure operations
deployer.rationale: Technical but well-defined work. Balanced models appropriate.

tester.description: Test coverage — writes unit, integration, and functionality tests
tester.rationale: Edge case judgment needed. Balanced models provide this at reasonable cost.

coder.description: Principal-level implementation — writes, modifies, and refactors production-quality code
coder.rationale: Execution-heavy reasoning over known specs. Balanced models handle large contexts well. Review-er is the quality backstop.

techwriter.description: Technical documentation — API docs, architecture documentation, installation guides
techwriter.rationale: Precision required but not high-stakes. Balanced models sufficient for accurate technical writing.

reviewer.description: Deep security and quality audit — finds subtle bugs, injection risks, and logic flaws
reviewer.rationale: Last line of defense. Must catch what others miss. Premium models justified for security work.

architecter.description: Infrastructure and architectural decisions — databases, frameworks, deployment patterns
architecter.rationale: High-stakes decisions with long-term consequences. Best models needed for trade-off evaluation.

reoriginator.description: Major refactoring — rewrites entire subsystems, restructures for new requirements
reoriginator.rationale: Large-scale restructuring with backward compatibility. Deep understanding required.

prosewriter.description: Clear communication — marketing content, blog posts, README files, announcements
prosewriter.rationale: User-facing content represents the project. Premium models excel at nuanced, clear writing.
